data.content:
# Implementation Plan — foolery-c751

Remove BD backend as implicit fallback default in auto backend resolution. Replace silent `?? "cli"` fallback with `DispatchFailureError`-style fail-loudly behaviour, so missing or unknown repo context surfaces a `FOOLERY DISPATCH FAILURE` banner instead of a misleading downstream `table not found: issues` error.

## Current State (evidence)

- `src/lib/backend-factory.ts:33` — `AutoRoutingBackend` constructor defaults `fallbackType = "cli"`.
- `src/lib/backend-factory.ts:38` — `resolveType(repoPath)` returns `this.fallbackType` when `!repoPath`.
- `src/lib/backend-factory.ts:49` — `resolveType` returns `this.fallbackType` when `detectMemoryManagerType` returns `undefined` (unknown / unsupported repo).
- `src/lib/backend-factory.ts:202` — `createBackend("auto")` instantiates `new AutoRoutingBackend("cli")`.
- `src/lib/memory-manager-detection.ts:8-16` — returns `undefined` when no marker directory found.
- `src/lib/dispatch-pool-resolver.ts:42-133` — existing `DispatchFailureError`, `DISPATCH_FAILURE_MARKER`, `emitDispatchFailureBanner` primitives. Currently shaped only for agent-pool dispatch (beatId/state/workflowId/poolKey).
- `src/lib/__tests__/backend-factory-full-coverage.test.ts:99,116` — tests that explicitly assert the silent fallback behaviour. These are the wrong contract and must change.
- `src/lib/backend-instance.ts:22` — `getBackend()` reads `FOOLERY_BACKEND` (defaults `"auto"`). Explicit `FOOLERY_BACKEND=cli|knots|beads|stub` is unaffected by this change.
- `src/lib/agent-identity.ts:465` — `?? "cli"` here defaults agent _type_, not backend selection. Out of scope.
- `src/lib/bd-internal.ts:23` — `BD_BIN ?? "bd"` defaults the binary name. Out of scope.

## Design

### 1. Extend the failure model — `src/lib/dispatch-pool-resolver.ts`

Convert `DispatchFailureInfo` to a discriminated union covering both agent-dispatch failures (existing) and backend-resolution failures (new):

```ts
export type DispatchFailureInfo =
  | AgentDispatchFailure
  | BackendDispatchFailure;

export interface AgentDispatchFailure {
  kind: "agent";
  beatId: string;
  state: string;
  workflowId: string;
  poolKey: string | null;
  reason: "no_pool_key" | "no_pool_configured" | "pool_empty" | "no_eligible_agent";
  excluded?: ReadonlyArray<string>;
}

export interface BackendDispatchFailure {
  kind: "backend";
  repoPath: string | null;
  method: string;          // e.g. "get", "list", "create"
  detectedType?: string;   // for future use (currently always undefined)
  reason: "repo_path_missing" | "repo_type_unknown";
}
```

Update `emitDispatchFailureBanner`, `DispatchFailureError` constructor message, and `remediationFor` to switch on `info.kind`.

Backend-kind remediation hints:
- `repo_path_missing` → "pass `_repo=<absolute path>` query/body or set the `repoPath` argument; the auto-router cannot pick a backend without it. To bypass auto-routing entirely, set `FOOLERY_BACKEND=cli|knots|beads`."
- `repo_type_unknown` → "no memory-manager marker (e.g., `.knots/`, `.beads/`) found in `<repoPath>`. Run `kno init` (knots) or `bd init` (beads), or set `FOOLERY_BACKEND=cli|knots|beads` to bypass detection."

Existing agent-dispatch call sites in this file (lines ~167–217) get a one-line edit each: add `kind: "agent"` to the literal info object. No behavioural change.

### 2. Make the auto-router throw — `src/lib/backend-factory.ts`

- Remove the `fallbackType` parameter from `AutoRoutingBackend` constructor.
- Change `resolveType(repoPath?)` → `resolveType(method: string, repoPath?)`:
  - `if (!repoPath)` → throw `new DispatchFailureError({ kind: "backend", repoPath: null, method, reason: "repo_path_missing" })`.
  - `const memoryManager = detectMemoryManagerType(repoPath)`.
  - if `memoryManager === "knots"` → `"knots"`.
  - else if `memoryManager === "beads"` → `"cli"`.
  - else → throw `new DispatchFailureError({ kind: "backend", repoPath, method, reason: "repo_type_unknown" })`.
- Each delegated method on `AutoRoutingBackend` (`get`, `list`, `search`, `create`, `update`, `delete`, `close`, `markTerminal`, `reopen`, `rewind`, `listDependencies`, `addDependency`, `removeDependency`, `buildTakePrompt`, `buildPollPrompt`, `query`, `listReady`) calls `backendFor(<method-name>, args[N])` so the failure banner names the operation.
- **Special case `listWorkflows(repoPath?)`**: when `!repoPath`, return `{ ok: true, data: builtinWorkflowDescriptors() }` directly — preserving the legitimate "show me builtin workflows" flow used by `GET /api/workflows` with no `_repo` query param. Import `builtinWorkflowDescriptors` from the existing builtin-workflow module (verify import path during implementation).
- `createBackend("auto")` → `new AutoRoutingBackend()` (no fallback arg).
- `capabilitiesForRepo(repoPath?)`: same try-throw semantics. UI callers must either supply a repoPath, catch the throw, or be repo-agnostic (using `getBackendCapabilities()` instead). Audit during implementation; if many UI sites would break, change `capabilitiesForRepo` alone to return `FULL_CAPABILITIES` on resolution failure (not the data path — only the capability path).

### 3. Surface failures in API routes

Add a helper `dispatchFailureToResponse(err: DispatchFailureError): NextResponse` to `src/lib/backend-http.ts`:

```ts
export function dispatchFailureToResponse(err: DispatchFailureError): NextResponse {
  return NextResponse.json(
    { error: err.message, banner: err.banner, marker: DISPATCH_FAILURE_MARKER },
    { status: 500 },
  );
}
```

Wrap each route handler body with `try { ... } catch (err) { if (err instanceof DispatchFailureError) return dispatchFailureToResponse(err); throw err; }`. Routes touched:

- `src/app/api/beats/[id]/route.ts` (GET, PATCH, DELETE)
- `src/app/api/beats/route.ts` (GET, POST)
- `src/app/api/beats/ready/route.ts`
- `src/app/api/beats/query/route.ts`
- `src/app/api/beats/merge/route.ts`
- `src/app/api/beats/batch-deps/route.ts`
- `src/app/api/beats/[id]/deps/route.ts`
- `src/app/api/beats/[id]/refine-scope/route.ts`
- `src/app/api/beats/[id]/close-cascade/route.ts`
- `src/app/api/beats/[id]/mark-terminal/route.ts`
- `src/app/api/beats/[id]/close/route.ts`
- `src/app/api/beats/[id]/reopen/route.ts`
- `src/app/api/beats/[id]/rewind/route.ts`
- `src/app/api/waves/route.ts`
- `src/app/api/workflows/route.ts` (catches the throw too, but should never be hit because of the listWorkflows short-circuit; catch defensively).

Server-side libs that call `getBackend()` (`regroom.ts`, `cascade-close.ts`, `orchestration-*.ts`, `terminal-manager-*.ts`, `scope-refinement-worker.ts`, `doctor-*.ts`, `beats-multi-repo.ts`, `memory-manager-commands.ts`) all already pass `repoPath` derived from their own context. If their context didn't have a repoPath, that's a real bug worth surfacing — leave them to throw naturally.

### 4. Tests — `src/lib/__tests__/`

**Update `backend-factory-full-coverage.test.ts`:**
- "resolves to fallback when no repoPath given" → rename "throws DispatchFailureError when no repoPath given"; assert `DispatchFailureError` with `info.kind === "backend"`, `info.reason === "repo_path_missing"`, message includes `"FOOLERY DISPATCH FAILURE"`.
- "resolves to fallback for unknown memory manager" → rename "throws DispatchFailureError for unknown memory manager"; assert `info.reason === "repo_type_unknown"`, `info.repoPath === "/repo"`.
- Delegated-method tests (`arb.list()`, `arb.get("x")`, etc., currently no-arg) must pass an explicit `repoPath` OR rely on the existing module-level `vi.mock("@/lib/memory-manager-detection", () => ({ detectMemoryManagerType: vi.fn(() => "beads") }))` — confirm the no-arg form throws now (it should), and switch tests to provide a repo path.
- Update `arb = new AutoRoutingBackend("cli")` → `arb = new AutoRoutingBackend()`.

**New file `src/lib/__tests__/backend-factory-no-fallback.test.ts`:**
1. `arb.get("id")` (no repoPath) → throws `DispatchFailureError` with `kind: "backend"`, `reason: "repo_path_missing"`, `method: "get"`.
2. `arb.list({}, "/unknown-repo")` with `detectMemoryManagerType` mocked to return `undefined` → throws with `reason: "repo_type_unknown"`, `repoPath: "/unknown-repo"`.
3. `arb.get("id", "/knots-repo")` with detection returning `"knots"` → routes to `KnotsBackend` (no throw).
4. `arb.get("id", "/beads-repo")` with detection returning `"beads"` → routes to BD (no throw).
5. `arb.listWorkflows()` (no repoPath) → returns builtin descriptors, no throw.
6. `arb.listWorkflows("/unknown")` with detection `undefined` → throws (still requires a known type once a path is provided).
7. Banner content check: error message contains `FOOLERY DISPATCH FAILURE`, contains the method name, contains the repoPath (or `<missing>`).

**Smoke test for the regression**:
- A focused test that boots a `BdCliBackend` against a missing/empty database surface — confirm pre-fix that `arb.get("id")` returned `table not found: issues` (the bad path), post-fix that the same call throws `DispatchFailureError`. May be reproducible via `getBackend()` in a tmp dir; defer to integration if too fiddly.

### 5. Documentation

- `CLAUDE.md` §"Fail Loudly, Never Silently": append a paragraph noting backend resolution as a covered case. Update the historical-incident section to mention the parallel BD-fallback incident this knot fixes.
- Search `docs/` for any line that says "if the repo is unknown, falls back to BD"; remove or correct.

## Acceptance Criteria Mapping

1. **No `?? "bd"` / `?? "cli"` / `Object.values(...)[0]` fallbacks**: verified by grep. Note: the `?? "cli"` in `src/lib/agent-identity.ts:465` is agent-_type_ defaulting and is out of scope. The `?? "bd"` in `src/lib/bd-internal.ts:23` is BD_BIN defaulting and is out of scope.
2. **`GET /api/beats/[id]` and beat-detail paths throw `DispatchFailureError` with marker**: addressed by sections 2+3 above. Verified by route-level test or manual curl.
3. **Knots → Knots backend; beads → BD backend**: covered by tests 3+4 in the new test file; existing `backend-factory-full-coverage.test.ts` "resolves to knots" / "resolves to cli for beads" tests remain green.
4. **New tests cover the no-fallback path; tests fail if BD is re-introduced as fallback**: tests 1+2 in the new file. Test 1 will fail if any code re-introduces `?? "cli"` because the throw will be silently replaced with a return.

## Risk & Complexity

**Complexity: medium.** Touches one core file (`backend-factory`), one type file (`dispatch-pool-resolver`), ~14 API routes (mechanical try/catch wrapper), 2 test files. ~150–250 LOC.

**Risks**:
1. **CLI usage outside a recognized repo**: any tooling that calls `getBackend()` from a directory with no `.knots/`/`.beads/` marker now hard-fails. Mitigation: the `FOOLERY_BACKEND=cli|knots|beads` env-var escape hatch already exists; document in the error remediation hint.
2. **`capabilitiesForRepo` callers in UI**: if the UI calls this with no/unknown repo, throws will crash render paths. Audit UI call sites first; if many, special-case `capabilitiesForRepo` to return `FULL_CAPABILITIES` on resolution failure (capabilities are advisory; data calls remain strict).
3. **`scope === "all"` multi-repo aggregation**: already passes per-repo paths from the registry, no change needed.
4. **Builtin workflow listing**: preserved by the `listWorkflows(undefined)` short-circuit in section 2.

## Verification

- `bun run lint`
- `bunx tsc --noEmit`
- `bun run test` (all backend-factory tests + new no-fallback tests)
- `bun run build`
- Manual: `bun run dev`, `curl http://localhost:3000/api/beats/foolery-c751` (no `_repo`), expect 500 with `FOOLERY DISPATCH FAILURE` body and red banner in stderr.
- Manual: `curl http://localhost:3000/api/workflows` (no `_repo`), expect 200 with builtin workflows.
- Grep gate: `rg '\?\? "cli"|fallbackType' src/lib/backend-factory.ts src/lib/backend-instance.ts src/lib/dispatch-pool-resolver.ts` returns no matches.

## Out of Scope

- `BD_BIN ?? "bd"` (binary name default, unrelated).
- `agent_type ?? "cli"` (agent-identity default, unrelated).
- Refactoring the `BdCliBackend` or removing `cli` as an explicitly-selectable backend type — explicit `FOOLERY_BACKEND=cli` continues to work.
- UI-side handling/styling of the new 500 response (separate cosmetic knot if desired).
